summary: A deliberately vulnerable Flask web application with extensive attack surface across authentication, injection, file operations, and deserialization. Hardcoded secrets, SQL injection in nearly every database query, no input validation, and direct command/code execution endpoints make this application critically insecure across all OWASP Top 10 categories.

surfaces.description: Login endpoint with SQL injection in authentication query (string formatting), plaintext password logging, predictable MD5 session tokens, and information disclosure in error messages
SQL injection page with two injection points: LIKE clause via username parameter and ORDER BY clause via order parameter, both using direct string interpolation
Reflected XSS via f-string template construction bypassing Jinja2 autoescaping, with injection in HTML, attribute, JavaScript, and CSS contexts
OS command injection via os.popen() and os.system() with unsanitized hostname and count parameters, allowing arbitrary command execution
Local file inclusion via arbitrary file read with no path validation, enabling access to /etc/passwd and other sensitive system files
Server-side template injection via user-controlled Jinja2 Template() instantiation, enabling RCE through __subclasses__ traversal
API authentication with SQL injection in credential check, weak HS256 JWT with hardcoded secret, password logged in audit trail, and API key exposure in response
IDOR vulnerability exposing any user's data without authorization check, plus SQL injection in user lookup query via unvalidated user_id
Server-side request forgery allowing arbitrary HTTP requests to internal services and cloud metadata endpoints with no URL validation
Direct arbitrary command execution via subprocess.run() with shell=True and user-supplied command string
Arbitrary Python code execution via eval() on user-supplied input, enabling full system compromise
Insecure deserialization via pickle.loads() on base64-decoded user input, enabling arbitrary code execution through crafted pickle payloads
Unsafe YAML deserialization using yaml.FullLoader which can instantiate arbitrary Python objects leading to code execution
XML External Entity (XXE) injection via ET.fromstring() without disabling external entity resolution, enabling file read and SSRF
Unrestricted file upload with no type validation and path traversal via filename, allowing arbitrary file write to the filesystem
Zip slip vulnerability via extractall() without path validation, enabling arbitrary file overwrite through crafted archive entries
Server-side template injection via unsandboxed Jinja2 Template() with user-controlled template string and context
LDAP injection via unsanitized username in filter string construction, enabling authentication bypass and data exfiltration
Open redirect with no URL validation, enabling phishing attacks by redirecting users to attacker-controlled domains
SQL injection in document search LIKE clause plus IDOR allowing access to any user's documents without authorization
Chained SQL injection and local file inclusion: injection in doc_id query retrieves arbitrary file_path, which is then read without validation
IDOR in audit log viewer allowing any authenticated user to view other users' logs via user_id parameter, plus SQL injection in log retrieval
Users table storing passwords in plaintext, hardcoded default credentials (admin/admin123, guest/guest), and API keys without encryption
Audit log table with SQL injection in INSERT via string formatting, logging sensitive data including passwords in cleartext
API log retrieval with IDOR (no auth check) and SQL injection via user_id parameter in log query
